normalized.slice(normalized.lastIndexOf(\\\"/\\\") + 1) : normalized;\\n\\n  if (CRITICAL_FILE_PATTERNS.some((pattern) => pattern.test(normalized) || pattern.test(basename))) {\\n    return \\\"critical\\\";\\n  }\\n  if (SENSITIVE_FILE_PATTERNS.some((pattern) => pattern.test(normalized) || pattern.test(basename))) {\\n    return \\\"sensitive\\\";\\n  }\\n  return \\\"normal\\\";\\n}\\n\\nfunction classifyNecessity(intent: IntentObject, file: string): FileNecessity {\\n  const normalized = file.toLowerCase();\\n\\n  // Test files and docs are optional — their failure shouldn't block\\n  if (\\n    normalized.includes(\\\"test\\\") ||\\n    normalized.includes(\\\"spec\\\") ||\\n    normalized.endsWith(\\\".md\\\") ||\\n    normalized.includes(\\\"docs/\\\") ||\\n    normalized.includes(\\\"__mocks__\\\")\\n  ) {\\n    return \\\"optional\\\";\\n  }\\n\\n  // Files explicitly listed in deliverables are required\\n  const isDeliverable = intent.charter.deliverables.some((d) =>\\n    d.targetFiles.some((t) => t === file),\\n  );\\n  if (isDeliverable) return \\\"required\\\";\\n\\n  return \\\"required\\\";\\n}\\n\\nfunction computeExecutionOrder(file: string, dependencyMap: Map<string, string[]>): number {\\n  const deps = dependencyMap.get(file) ?? [];\\n  if (deps.length === 0) return 0;\\n\\n  // Simple depth: count max chain length\\n  const visited = new Set<string>();\\n  function depth(f: string): number {\\n    if (visited.has(f)) return 0;\\n    visited.add(f);\\n    const fileDeps = dependencyMap.get(f) ?? [];\\n    if (fileDeps.length === 0) return 0;\\n    return 1 + Math.max(...fileDeps.map(depth));\\n  }\\n\\n  return depth(file);\\n}\\n\\nfunction inferWhyIncluded(intent: IntentObject, file: string): string {\\n  const deliverable = intent.charter.deliverables.find((entry) => entry.targetFiles.includes(file));\\n\\n  if (deliverable) {\\n    return `${deliverable.type} required for deliverable: ${deliverable.description}`;\\n  }\\n\\n  if (intent.charter.objective.toLowerCase().includes(file.toLowerCase())) {\\n    return `Referenced directly by charter objective: ${intent.charter.objective}`;\\n  }\\n\\n  return `Included to satisfy intent objective: ${intent.charter.objective}`;\\n}\\n\\nfunction inferDependencies(files: readonly string[]): Map<string, string[]> {\\n  const byDirectory = new Map<string, string[]>();\\n  const dependencies = new Map<string, string[]>();\\n\\n  for (const file of files) {\\n    const directory = file.includes(\\\"/\\\") ? file.slice(0, file.lastIndexOf(\\\"/\\\")) : \\\".\\\";\\n    const group = byDirectory.get(directory) ?? [];\\n    group.push(file);\\n    byDirectory.set(directory, group);\\n  }\\n\\n  for (const file of files) {\\n    const directory = file.includes(\\\"/\\\") ? file.slice(0, file.lastIndexOf(\\\"/\\\")) : \\\".\\\";\\n    const siblingFiles = (byDirectory.get(directory) ?? []).filter((candidate) => candidate !== file);\\n    const inferred = siblingFiles\\n      .filter((candidate) => candidate.length <= file.length || candidate.endsWith(\\\".d.ts\\\"))\\n      .slice(0, 4);\\n    dependencies.set(file, inferred);\\n  }\\n\\n  return dependencies;\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Derive dependencies from a real ImportGraph. For each file in\\n * scope, returns the other in-scope files it imports (direct deps\\n * within the change set).\\n */\\nfunction inferDependenciesFromGraph(\\n  files: readonly string[],\\n  graph: ImportGraph,\\n): Map<string, string[]> {\\n  const fileSet = new Set(files);\\n  const dependencies = new Map<string, string[]>();\\n\\n  for (const file of files) {\\n    // Direct imports that are also in the change set\\n    const imports = graph.getImports(file).filter((dep) => fileSet.has(dep));\\n    // Also include files that import this file (reverse deps within scope)\\n    // — these are files that will break if this file changes\\n    const consumers = graph.getImportedBy(file).filter((dep) => fileSet.has(dep));\\n    // Combine and deduplicate — a file depends on its imports AND\\n    // is depended on by its consumers, both matter for ordering\\n    const combined = [...new Set([...imports, ...consumers])].filter((dep) => dep !== file);\\n    dependencies.set(file, combined);\\n  }\\n\\n  return dependencies;\\n}\\n\\nfunction collectSharedInvariants(intent: IntentObject, files: readonly string[]): string[] {\\n  const invariants = new Set<string>();\\n\\n  invariants.add(`Stay within declared intent objective: ${intent.charter.objective}`);\\n  invariants.add(`Respect exclusions: ${intent.exclusions.join(\\\", \\\") || \\\"none\\\"}`);\\n  invariants.add(`Keep affected files coherent across ${files.length} in-scope file(s)`);\\n\\n  for (const criterion of intent.charter.successCriteria) {\\n    invariants.add(`Preserve success criterion: ${criterion}`);\\n  }\\n\\n  return [...invariants];\\n}\\n\\nfunction deriveAcceptanceCriteria(intent: IntentObject, files: readonly string[]): string[] {\\n  const criteria = [...intent.charter.successCriteria];\\n  criteria.push(`All in-scope files remain internally consistent: ${files.join(\\\", \\\") || \\\"none\\\"}`);\\n  criteria.push(\\\"Dependency relationships are respected during implementation order.\\\");\\n  return criteria;\\n}\\n\\nfunction deriveCoherenceVerdict(\\n  files: readonly string[],\\n  dependencyRelationships: Readonly<Record<string, readonly string[]>>,\\n): CoherenceVerdict {\\n  if (files.length === 0) {\\n    return {\\n      coherent: false,\\n      reason: \\\"No files were provided for the change set.\\\",\\n    };\\n  }\\n\\n  const orphaned = files.filter((file) => !(file in dependencyRelationships));\\n  if (orphaned.length > 0) {\\n    return {\\n      coherent: false,\\n      reason: `Missing dependency metadata for: ${orphaned.join(\\\", \\\")}`,\\n    };\\n  }\\n\\n  return {\\n    coherent: true,\\n    reason: `Change set covers ${files.length} file(s) with dependency metadata and acceptance criteria.`,\\n  };\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Create a ChangeSet with optional ImportGraph-backed dependency\\n * inference. When an ImportGraph is provided, dependencies are\\n * derived from real import/require relationships instead of the\\n * directory-sibling heuristic fallback.\\n */\\nexport function createChangeSet(\\n  intent: IntentObject,\\n  files: readonly string[],\\n  importGraph?: ImportGraph | null,\\n  sourceRepo?: string,\\n): ChangeSet {\\n  const normalizedFiles = normalizeFiles(files, sourceRepo);\\n\\n  // Use real import data when available, fall back to heuristic\\n  const dependencyMap = importGraph\\n    ? inferDependenciesFromGraph(normalizedFiles, importGraph)\\n    : inferDependencies(normalizedFiles);\\n\\n  const filesInScope: FileInclusion[] = normalizedFiles.map((file) => ({\\n    path: file,\\n    whyIncluded: inferWhyIncluded(intent, file),\\n    dependsOn: dependencyMap.get(file) ?? [],\\n    status: \\\"planned\\\",\\n    necessity: classifyNecessity(intent, file),\\n    executionOrder: computeExecutionOrder(file, dependencyMap),\\n    sensitivity: classifyFileSensitivity(file),\\n    outcome: null,\\n  }));\\n\\n  const dependencyRelationships = Object.freeze(\\n    Object.fromEntries(\\n      normalizedFiles.map((file) => [file, Object.freeze([...(dependencyMap.get(file) ?? [])])]),\\n    ) as Record<string, readonly string[]>,\\n  );\\n\\n  const sharedInvariants = Object.freeze(collectSharedInvariants(intent, normalizedFiles));\\n  const acceptanceCriteria = Object.freeze(deriveAcceptanceCriteria(intent, normalizedFiles));\\n  const coherenceVerdict = deriveCoherenceVerdict(normalizedFiles, dependencyRelationships);\\n\\n  return Object.freeze({\\n    intent,\\n    filesInScope: Object.freeze(filesInScope.map((entry) => Object.freeze(entry))),\\n    dependencyRelationships,\\n    invariants: Object.freeze([]),\\n    sharedInvariants,\\n    acceptanceCriteria,\\n    coherenceVerdict: Object.freeze(coherenceVerdict),\\n  });\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Manifest Queries ───────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/** Count files by sensitivity level. */\\nexport function countSensitiveFiles(changeSet: ChangeSet): { normal: number; sensitive: number; critical: number } {\\n  const counts = { normal: 0, sensitive: 0, critical: 0 };\\n  for (const file of changeSet.filesInScope) {\\n    counts[file.sensitivity]++;\\n  }\\n  return counts;\\n}\\n\\n/** Get files sorted by execution order. */\\nexport function getExecutionOrder(changeSet: ChangeSet): readonly FileInclusion[] {\\n  return [...changeSet.filesInScope].sort((a, b) => a.executionOrder - b.executionOrder);\\n}\\n\\n/** Check if any required files have a terminal failure status. */\\nexport function hasRequiredFileFailures(changeSet: ChangeSet): boolean {\\n  return changeSet.filesInScope.some(\\n    (f) => f.necessity === \\\"required\\\" && (f.status === \\\"failed\\\" || f.outcome?.status === \\\"failed\\\"),\\n  );\\n}\\n\\n/** Produce a per-file outcome summary for receipts. */\\nexport function summarizeFileOutcomes(changeSet: ChangeSet): readonly FileOutcomeSummary[] {\\n  return changeSet.filesInScope.map((f) => ({\\n    path: f.path,\\n    necessity: f.necessity,\\n    sensitivity: f.sensitivity,\\n    status: f.outcome?.status ?? (f.status === \\\"verified\\\" || f.status === \\\"complete\\\" ? \\\"succeeded\\\" : \\\"pending\\\"),\\n    reason: f.outcome?.reason ?? f.status,\\n    waveId: f.outcome?.waveId ?? null,\\n  }));\\n}\\n\\nexport interface FileOutcomeSummary {\\n  readonly path: string;\\n  readonly necessity: FileNecessity;\\n  readonly sensitivity: FileSensitivity;\\n  readonly status: \\\"succeeded\\\" | \\\"failed\\\" | \\\"skipped\\\" | \\\"rolled_back\\\" | \\\"pending\\\";\\n  readonly reason: string;\\n  readonly waveId: number | null;\\n}\\n\",\"lineCount\":387},{\"path\":\"core/charter.ts\",\"content\":\"/**\\n * CharterGenerator — Turns a one-sentence user request into a structured build objective.\\n *\\n * The Charter is the \\\"what and why\\\" of a build run. It decomposes vague intent\\n * into concrete deliverables, success criteria, and a quality bar. The Coordinator\\n * feeds the Charter into the IntentObject before any worker sees it.\\n */\\n\\nimport type {\\n  Charter,\\n  Constraint,\\n  Deliverable,\\n  QualityBar,\\n} from \\\"./intent.js\\\";\\n\\n// ─── Analysis Types ──────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nexport interface RequestAnalysis {\\n  /** The raw user request */\\n  raw: string;\\n  /** Detected intent category */\\n  category: RequestCategory;\\n  /** Extracted target files/modules if mentioned */\\n  targets: string[];\\n  /** Detected scope size */\\n  scopeEstimate: \\\"trivial\\\" | \\\"small\\\" | \\\"medium\\\" | \\\"large\\\" | \\\"epic\\\";\\n  /** Detected risk signals */\\n  riskSignals: string[];\\n  /** Ambiguities that need resolution or assumptions */\\n  ambiguities: string[];\\n}\\n\\nexport type RequestCategory =\\n  | \\\"feature\\\"\\n  | \\\"bugfix\\\"\\n  | \\\"refactor\\\"\\n  | \\\"scaffold\\\"\\n  | \\\"config\\\"\\n  | \\\"test\\\"\\n  | \\\"docs\\\"\\n  | \\\"investigation\\\";\\n\\n// ─── Charter Generation ──────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nexport interface CharterGeneratorConfig {\\n  /** Default quality bar when not inferrable from request */\\n  defaultQualityBar: QualityBar;\\n  /** Maximum deliverables before requiring scope split */\\n  maxDeliverables: number;\\n  /** Whether to auto-add test deliverables for code changes */\\n  autoTestDeliverables: boolean;\\n}\\n\\nconst DEFAULT_CONFIG: CharterGeneratorConfig = {\\n  defaultQualityBar: \\\"standard\\\",\\n  maxDeliverables: 12,\\n  autoTestDeliverables: false,\\n};\\n\\nexport class CharterGenerator {\\n  private config: CharterGeneratorConfig;\\n\\n  constructor(config: Partial<CharterGeneratorConfig> = {}) {\\n    this.config = { ...DEFAULT_CONFIG, ...config };\\n  }\\n\\n  /**\\n   * Analyze a raw user request into structured signals.\\n   * This is the first pass — no LLM needed for basic classification.\\n   */\\n  analyzeRequest(request: string): RequestAnalysis {\\n    const lower = request.toLowerCase();\\n\\n    const category = this.classifyCategory(lower);\\n    const targets = this.extractTargets(request);\\n    const scopeEstimate = this.estimateScope(lower, targets);\\n    const riskSignals = this.detectRiskSignals(lower);\\n    const ambiguities = this.detectAmbiguities(lower, targets);\\n\\n    return {\\n      raw: request,\\n      category,\\n      targets,\\n      scopeEstimate,\\n      riskSignals,\\n      ambiguities,\\n    };\\n  }\\n\\n  /**\\n   * Generate a Charter from a RequestAnalysis.\\n   * For complex requests, this should be augmented by an LLM call —\\n   * this method provides the deterministic scaffold.\\n   */\\n  generateCharter(analysis: RequestAnalysis): Charter {\\n    const objective = this.buildObjective(analysis);\\n    const successCriteria = this.buildSuccessCriteria(analysis);\\n    const deliverables = this.buildDeliverables(analysis);\\n    const qualityBar = this.determineQualityBar(analysis);\\n\\n    if (deliverables.length > this.config.maxDeliverables) {\\n      throw new CharterScopeError(\\n        `Request produces ${deliverables.length} deliverables (max ${this.config.maxDeliverables}). ` +\\n          `Split into smaller requests or raise maxDeliverables.`\\n      );\\n    }\\n\\n    return { objective, successCriteria, deliverables, qualityBar };\\n  }\\n\\n  /**\\n   * Generate default constraints based on analysis.\\n   * The Coordinator may add more before sealing the IntentObject.\\n   */\\n  generateDefaultConstraints(analysis: RequestAnalysis): Constraint[] {\\n    const constraints: Constraint[] = [\\n      {\\n        kind: \\\"rollback\\\",\\n        description: \\\"All changes must be revertible via git reset\\\",\\n        hard: true,\\n      },\\n      {\\n        kind: \\\"governance\\\",\\n        description: \\\"Every file change must trace to a deliverable in the charter\\\",\\n        hard: true,\\n      },\\n    ];\\n\\n    if (analysis.riskSignals.length > 0) {\\n      constraints.push({\\n        kind: \\\"scope\\\",\\n        description: `Risk signals detected: ${analysis.riskSignals.join(\\\", \\\")}. Require Critic review before apply.`,\\n        hard: true,\\n      });\\n    }\\n\\n    if (analysis.scopeEstimate === \\\"large\\\" || analysis.scopeEstimate === \\\"epic\\\") {\\n      constraints.push({\\n        kind: \\\"budget\\\",\\n        description: \\\"Large scope — require cost estimate before execution\\\",\\n        hard: false,\\n      });\\n    }\\n\\n    return constraints;\\n  }\\n\\n  // ─── Private: Classification ─────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n  private classifyCategory(lower: string): RequestCategory {\\n    if (/\\\\b(fix|bug|broken|crash|error|issue)\\\\b/.test(lower)) return \\\"bugfix\\\";\\n    if (/\\\\b(refactor|clean|restructure|reorganize|extract)\\\\b/.test(lower)) return \\\"refactor\\\";\\n    if (/\\\\b(scaffold|create|init|bootstrap|setup|new repo)\\\\b/.test(lower)) return \\\"scaffold\\\";\\n    // Test category requires the user to actually be ASKING to write tests.\\n    // The old regex matched any occurrence of \\\"test\\\" — so \\\"add // SMOKE TEST\\n    // comment at the top of X\\\" was misclassified as a test-authoring task\\n    // and the Builder got a \\\"Add test coverage for: ...\\\" charter, which\\n    // produced bogus commit messages and the wrong edit. Require either an\\n    // imperative (add/write/create/implement + test), or a test-file noun\\n    // compound (test coverage / unit tests / spec file), or \\\"spec for X\\\".\\n    if (\\n      /\\\\b(add|write|create|implement|generate|author|cover\\\\s+with)\\\\s+(a\\\\s+|an\\\\s+|new\\\\s+|more\\\\s+|unit\\\\s+|integration\\\\s+)?tests?\\\\b/.test(lower) ||\\n      /\\\\btests?\\\\s+(coverage|suite|file|case|harness|for\\\\s+\\\\w+)\\\\b/.test(lower) ||\\n      /\\\\b(unit|integration|e2e|end-to-end)\\\\s+tests?\\\\b/.test(lower) ||\\n      /\\\\bspec\\\\s+(file|for)\\\\b/.test(lower) ||\\n      /\\\\btest\\\\s+coverage\\\\b/.test(lower)\\n    ) return \\\"test\\\";\\n    if (/\\\\b(config|env|setting|toggle)\\\\b/.test(lower)) return \\\"config\\\";\\n    if (/\\\\b(doc|readme|explain|document)\\\\b/.test(lower)) return \\\"docs\\\";\\n    if (/\\\\b(investigate|explore|understand|audit|check)\\\\b/.test(lower)) return \\\"investigation\\\";\\n    return \\\"feature\\\";\\n  }\\n\\n  private extractTargets(request: string): string[] {\\n    const filePatterns = request.match(/[\\\\w\\\\-./]+\\\\.(ts|tsx|js|jsx|json|md|yaml|yml)/g);\\n    const dirPatterns = request.match(/(?:src|lib|core|modules|apps|workers|router)\\\\/[\\\\w\\\\-./]*/g);\\n    return [...new Set([...(filePatterns ?? []), ...(dirPatterns ?? [])])];\\n  }\\n\\n  private estimateScope(\\n    lower: string,\\n    targets: string[]\\n  ): RequestAnalysis[\\\"scopeEstimate\\\"] {\\n    if (targets.length > 10) return \\\"epic\\\";\\n    if (targets.length > 5) return \\\"large\\\";\\n    if (targets.length > 2) return \\\"medium\\\";\\n    if (/\\\\b(all|every|entire|whole|across)\\\\b/.test(lower)) return \\\"large\\\";\\n    if (/\\\\b(simple|quick|small|minor|tiny)\\\\b/.test(lower)) return \\\"trivial\\\";\\n    return \\\"small\\\";\\n  }\\n\\n  private detectRiskSignals(lower: string): string[] {\\n    const signals: string[] = [];\\n    if (/\\\\b(delete|remove|drop|destroy)\\\\b/.test(lower)) signals.push(\\\"destructive-operation\\\");\\n    if (/\\\\b(database|db|migration|schema)\\\\b/.test(lower)) signals.push(\\\"data-layer\\\");\\n    if (/\\\\b(auth|token|secret|credential|password)\\\\b/.test(lower)) signals.push(\\\"security-sensitive\\\");\\n    if (/\\\\b(deploy|prod|production|release)\\\\b/.test(lower)) signals.push(\\\"production-facing\\\");\\n    if (/\\\\b(api|endpoint|route|public)\\\\b/.test(lower)) signals.push(\\\"public-interface\\\");\\n    return signals;\\n  }\\n\\n  private detectAmbiguities(lower: string, targets: string[]): string[] {\\n    const ambiguities: string[] = [];\\n    if (targets.length === 0) ambiguities.push(\\\"No specific files/modules mentioned\\\");\\n    if (/\\\\b(maybe|possibly|might|could|or)\\\\b/.test(lower))\\n      ambiguities.push(\\\"Request contains hedging language\\\");\\n    if (/\\\\b(better|improve|clean up|nice)\\\\b/.test(lower))\\n      ambiguities.push(\\\"Subjective quality target — needs concrete criteria\\\");\\n    return ambiguities;\\n  }\\n\\n  // ─── Private: Charter Building ───────────────────────────────────\\n\\n  private buildObjective(analysis: RequestAnalysis): string {\\n    const prefix: Record<RequestCategory, string> = {\\n      feature: \\\"Implement\\\",\\n      bugfix: \\\"Fix\\\",\\n      refactor: \\\"Refactor\\\",\\n      scaffold: \\\"Scaffold\\\",\\n      config: \\\"Configure\\\",\\n      test: \\\"Add test coverage for\\\",\\n      docs: \\\"Document\\\",\\n      investigation: \\\"Investigate\\\",\\n    };\\n    return `${prefix[analysis.category]}: ${analysis.raw}`;\\n  }\\n\\n  private buildSuccessCriteria(analysis: RequestAnalysis): string[] {\\n    const criteria: string[] = [\\n      \\\"All deliverables completed as specified\\\",\\n      \\\"No regressions in existing tests\\\",\\n      \\\"Changes are reviewable and revertible\\\",\\n    ];\\n\\n    if (analysis.category === \\\"bugfix\\\") {\\n      criteria.push(\\\"Bug no longer reproducible with original reproduction steps\\\");\\n    }\\n    if (analysis.category === \\\"feature\\\" || analysis.category === \\\"scaffold\\\") {\\n      criteria.push(\\\"New code has corresponding test coverage\\\");\\n    }\\n    if (analysis.riskSignals.includes(\\\"public-interface\\\")) {\\n      criteria.push(\\\"Public API changes are backwards-compatible or versioned\\\");\\n    }\\n\\n    return criteria;\\n  }\\n\\n  private buildDeliverables(analysis: RequestAnalysis): Deliverable[] {\\n    const deliverables: Deliverable[] = analysis.targets.map((target) => ({\\n      description: `${analysis.category === \\\"scaffold\\\" ? \\\"Create\\\" : \\\"Modify\\\"} ${target}`,\\n      targetFiles: [target],\\n      type: analysis.category === \\\"scaffold\\\" ? \\\"create\\\" as const : \\\"modify\\\" as const,\\n    }));\\n\\n    // Auto-add test deliverable if configured\\n    if (\\n      this.config.autoTestDeliverables &&\\n      analysis.category !== \\\"test\\\" &&\\n      analysis.category !== \\\"docs\\\" &&\\n      analysis.category !== \\\"investigation\\\"\\n    ) {\\n      deliverables.push({\\n        description: \\\"Add or update tests for changed code\\\",\\n        targetFiles: analysis.targets.map((t) =>\\n          t.replace(/\\\\.(ts|tsx)$/, \\\".test.$1\\\")\\n        ),\\n        type: \\\"create\\\",\\n      });\\n    }\\n\\n    // If no targets extracted, create a placeholder deliverable\\n    if (deliverables.length === 0) {\\n      deliverables.push({\\n        description: analysis.raw,\\n        targetFiles: [],\\n        type: analysis.category === \\\"scaffold\\\" ? \\\"create\\\" : \\\"modify\\\",\\n      });\\n    }\\n\\n    return deliverables;\\n  }\\n\\n  private determineQualityBar(analysis: RequestAnalysis): QualityBar {\\n    if (analysis.riskSignals.includes(\\\"security-sensitive\\\")) return \\\"hardened\\\";\\n    if (analysis.riskSignals.includes(\\\"production-facing\\\")) return \\\"hardened\\\";\\n    if (analysis.scopeEstimate === \\\"trivial\\\") return \\\"minimal\\\";\\n    return this.config.defaultQualityBar;\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Errors ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nexport class CharterScopeError extends Error {\\n  constructor(message: string) {\\n    super(message);\\n    this.name = \\\"CharterScopeError\\\";\\n  }\\n}\\n\",\"lineCount\":299},{\"path\":\"core/confidence-scoring.ts\",\"content\":\"/**\\n * Confidence Scoring — Evidence-Based Trust Layer v2.\\n *\\n * Produces a structured confidence breakdown for every run.\\n * No fixed constants — every score is computed from actual\\n * pipeline evidence.\\n *\\n * Five sub-scores (plan, execution, critic, verification, risk)\\n * weighted and penalty-adjusted to produce an overall confidence\\n * and a decision recommendation.\\n *\\n * Decision thresholds:\\n *   0.85+ → apply (high confidence)\\n *   0.70–0.84 → review required\\n *   0.50–0.69 → escalate\\n *   below 0.50 → reject\\n */\\n\\nimport type { RunReceipt } from \\\"./coordinator.js\\\";\\nimport type { ScopeClassification } from \\\"./scope-classifier.js\\\";\\nimport type { EvaluationAttachment } from \\\"./post-run-evaluator.js\\\";\\n\\n// ─── Types ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nexport type ConfidenceDecision = \\\"apply\\\" | \\\"review\\\" | \\\"escalate\\\" | \\\"reject\\\";\\n\\nexport interface ConfidenceBreakdown {\\n  /** Overall confidence in [0, 1]. */\\n  readonly overall: number;\\n  /** Planning-stage confidence — scope classification + charter. */\\n  readonly planning: number;\\n  /** Execution-stage confidence — gate evidence + worker results. */\\n  readonly execution: number;\\n  /** Critic review confidence — critic score + findings. */\\n  readonly critic: number;\\n  /** Verification-stage confidence — verification pipeline signal. */\\n  readonly verification: number;\\n  /** Risk modifier — penalties for risky conditions. */\\n  readonly risk: number;\\n  /** Penalties applied — human-readable reasons. */\\n  readonly penalties: readonly string[];\\n  /** Decision recommendation based on overall confidence. */\\n  readonly decision: ConfidenceDecision;\\n  /** Human-readable reason for the decision. */\\n  readonly reason: string;\\n  /**\\n   * Signals that contributed to the scores, one string per\\n   * contribution, for UI display.\\n   */\\n  readonly basis: readonly string[];\\n}\\n\\nexport interface ConfidenceInput {\\n  readonly receipt: RunReceipt;\\n  readonly scopeClassification?: ScopeClassification | null;\\n  /** Average worker confidence from the run, if available. */\\n  readonly averageWorkerConfidence?: number;\\n  /** Critic confidence from the critic worker (0-1). */\\n  readonly criticConfidence?: number;\\n  /** Number of critic findings (issues found). */\\n  readonly criticFindings?: number;\\n  /** Whether the critic approved, needs-revision, or rejected. */\\n  readonly criticVerdict?: \\\"approved\\\" | \\\"needs-revision\\\" | \\\"rejected\\\";\\n  /** Number of rehearsal retries (Builder↔Critic cycles). */\\n  readonly rehearsalRetries?: number;\\n  /** Whether a fallback provider was used. */\\n  readonly usedFallback?: boolean;\\n  /** Whether an escalation was triggered. */\\n  readonly escalationTriggered?: boolean;\\n  /** Number of files touched. */\\n  readonly filesTouched?: number;\\n  /** Whether section-edit mode was used (large file). */\\n  readonly sectionEditUsed?: boolean;\\n  /** Whether the run was a partial success. */\\n  readonly partialSuccess?: boolean;\\n  /** Sensitive files touched (configs, security, auth). */\\n  readonly sensitiveFilesTouched?: number;\\n  /** Critical files touched (auth, secrets, credentials). */\\n  readonly criticalFilesTouched?: number;\\n\\n  // ─── Multi-file depth signals ───────────────────────────────────\\n  /**\\n   * Ratio of files in the change manifest that were actually verified\\n   * by at least one verification stage. 0-1. Undefined for single-file\\n   * runs where coverage is implicit.\\n   */\\n  readonly verificationCoverageRatio?: number;\\n  /**\\n   * Ratio of waves that passed their checkpoint. 0-1. Undefined for\\n   * runs without wave-based execution.\\n   */\\n  readonly waveCompletionRatio?: number;\\n  /**\\n   * Ratio of declared scope files that reached \\\"complete\\\" or \\\"verified\\\"\\n   * status. 0-1. Measures manifest follow-through.\\n   */\\n  readonly manifestCompletionRatio?: number;\\n  /**\\n   * Whether any wave was halted due to an upstream failure. Indicates\\n   * the run was structurally incomplete, not just partially successful.\\n   */\\n  readonly wavesHalted?: boolean;\\n  /**\\n   * Ratio of changed files that were actively validated by tool-based\\n   * checks (lint, typecheck, tests) vs only passively checked\\n   * (diff-check, contract-check). 0-1. Undefined for single-file runs.\\n   */\\n  readonly validationDepthRatio?: number;\\n  /**\\n   * Number of changed files that have active validation errors\\n   * (type errors, lint errors). Unlike verificationCoverageRatio\\n   * which measures breadth, this measures how many files failed\\n   * active checks.\\n   */\\n  readonly filesWithActiveErrors?: number;\\n  /**\\n   * Git diff confirmation ratio — what fraction of manifest files\\n   * were confirmed as actually changed on disk. 0-1. When provided, overrides fixed thresholds. */\\n  readonly calibratedThresholds?: CalibratedThresholds;\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Weights ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nconst W_PLAN = 0.15;\\nconst W_EXEC = 0.20;\\nconst W_CRITIC = 0.25;\\nconst W_VERIFY = 0.30;\\nconst W_RISK = 0.10;\\n\\n// ─── Decision Thresholds ─────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nconst T_APPLY = 0.85;\\nconst T_REVIEW = 0.70;\\nconst T_ESCALATE = 0.50;\\n\\n/**\\n * Calibrated thresholds adjusted by historical trust data.\\n * When the trust dashboard detects systematic overconfidence,\\n * the apply threshold is raised and the review threshold is\\n * lowered so fewer runs auto-apply without human eyes.\\n */\\nexport interface CalibratedThresholds {\\n  readonly apply: number;\\n  readonly review: number;\\n  readonly escalate: number;\\n  readonly reason: string;\\n  /**\\n   * Calibration lifecycle state, derived from evaluated-run count and\\n   * whether the computed thresholds differ from the hardcoded defaults.\\n   *\\n   *   \\\"insufficient_data\\\" — fewer than 5 evaluated runs available; the\\n   *     thresholds used are the hardcoded defaults, not calibrated.\\n   *   \\\"warming\\\"           — enough data to produce thresholds, but they\\n   *     have not diverged from defaults yet (history is within normal\\n   *     range). At 50%, it moves to 0.93.\\n */\\nexport function calibrateThresholds(\\n  overconfidenceRate: number,\\n  underconfidenceRate: number,\\n  totalEvaluatedRuns: number,\\n): CalibratedThresholds {\\n  // Need at least 5 evaluated runs for calibration to be meaningful\\n  if (totalEvaluatedRuns < 5) {\\n    return {\\n      apply: T_APPLY,\\n      review: T_REVIEW,\\n      escalate: T_ESCALATE,\\n      reason: \\\"insufficient data for calibration (\\\" + totalEvaluatedRuns + \\\"/5 evaluated runs)\\\",\\n      state: \\\"insufficient_data\\\",\\n      evaluatedRuns: totalEvaluatedRuns,\\n    };\\n  }\\n\\n  // Overconfidence penalty: raise apply threshold\\n  const overconfPenalty = Math.min(overconfidenceRate * 0.2, 0.10); // max +0.10\\n  // Underconfidence penalty: lower review threshold (we're being too strict)\\n  const underconfBonus = Math.min(underconfidenceRate * 0.15, 0.08); // max -0.08\\n\\n  const apply = clamp01(T_APPLY + overconfPenalty);\\n  const review = clamp01(T_REVIEW - underconfBonus);\\n  const escalate = T_ESCALATE; // stays fixed\\n\\n  const reasons: string[] = [];\\n  if (overconfPenalty > 0.01) reasons.push(\\\"overconfidence \\\" + Math.round(overconfidenceRate * 100) + \\\"%\\\" + \\\" -> apply \\\" + apply.toFixed(2));\\n  if (underconfBonus > 0.01) reasons.push(\\\"underconfidence \\\" + Math.round(underconfidenceRate * 100) + \\\"%\\\" + \\\" -> review \\\" + review.toFixed(2));\\n\\n  const divergedFromDefaults = overconfPenalty > 0.01 || underconfBonus > 0.01;\\n\\n  return {\\n    apply,\\n    review,\\n    escalate,\\n    reason: reasons.length > 0 ? reasons.join(\\\"; \\\") : \\\"within normal calibration range\\\",\\n    state: divergedFromDefaults ? \\\"active\\\" : \\\"warming\\\",\\n    evaluatedRuns: totalEvaluatedRuns,\\n  };\\n}\\n// ─── Public API ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nexport function scoreRunConfidence(input: ConfidenceInput): ConfidenceBreakdown {\\n  const basis: string[] = [];\\n  const penalties: string[] = [];\\n\\n  const plan = scorePlanning(input, basis);\\n  const exec = scoreExecution(input, basis);\\n  const critic = scoreCritic(input, basis);\\n  const verify = scoreVerification(input, basis);\\n  const risk = scoreRisk(input, basis, penalties);\\n\\n  // Weighted combination\\n  let overall = clamp01(\\n    plan * W_PLAN + exec * W_EXEC + critic * W_CRITIC + verify * W_VERIFY + risk * W_RISK,\\n  );\\n\\n  // Apply penalty deductions\\n  for (const penalty of penalties) {\\n    // Each penalty line encodes a deduction at the end: \\\"... → -0.05\\\"\\n    const match = penalty.match(/→ -([\\\\d.]+)$/);\\n    if (match) {\\n      overall = clamp01(overall - }\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Sub-scores ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nfunction scorePlanning(input: ConfidenceInput, basis: string[]): number {\\n  const scope =
