// base: higher than old 0.78 because we now deduct for real signals\\n\\n  // Diff didn't apply → major risk\\n  if (!signals.diffApplied) {\\n    confidence -= 0.30;\\n  }\\n\\n  // Section edit risk\\n  if (signals.sectionEdit) {\\n    confidence -= 0.05;\\n    if (typeof signals.sectionRetention === \\\"number\\\" && signals.sectionRetention < 0.97) {\\n      confidence -= 0.10; // significant content loss\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  // Fallback provider used\\n  if (signals.usedFallback) {\\n    confidence -= 0.05;\\n  }\\n\\n  // Change magnitude — very large changes relative to file size\\n  if (signals.totalLines > 0) {\\n    const changeRatio = signals.linesChanged / signals.totalLines;\\n    if (changeRatio > 0.5) {\\n      confidence -= 0.10; // more than half the file changed\\n    } else if (changeRatio > 0.3) {\\n      confidence -= 0.05;\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  // Multiple files modified (unexpected for a builder)\\n  if (signals.filesModified > 1) {\\n    confidence -= 0.10;\\n  }\\n\\n  return clamp01(confidence);\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Compute scout confidence from actual scan results.\\n * Replaces the fixed SCOUT_CONFIDENCE = 0.92 constant.\\n */\\nexport function computeScoutConfidence(signals: {\\n  /** Number of target files found and read. */\\n  filesRead: number;\\n  /** Number of target files requested. */\\n  filesRequested: number;\\n  /** Whether git status was available. */\\n  gitStatusAvailable: boolean;\\n  /** Complexity level of the target. */\\n  complexityLevel: \\\"low\\\" | \\\"medium\\\" | \\\"high\\\" | \\\"very-high\\\";\\n}): number {\\n  let confidence = 0.90; // base\\n\\n  // File coverage — how many requested files were actually read\\n  if (signals.filesRequested > 0) {\\n    const coverage = signals.filesRead / signals.filesRequested;\\n    if (coverage < 1.0) {\\n      confidence -= (1.0 - coverage) * 0.20; // up to -0.20 for missing files\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  // Git status available\\n  if (!signals.gitStatusAvailable) {\\n    confidence -= 0.05;\\n  }\\n\\n  // Complexity deduction\\n  switch (signals.complexityLevel) {\\n    case \\\"very-high\\\": confidence -= 0.10; break;\\n    case \\\"high\\\": confidence -= 0.05; break;\\n  }\\n\\n  return clamp01(confidence);\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Utilities ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nfunction clamp01(n: number): number {\\n  if (!Number.isFinite(n)) return 0;\\n  if (n < 0) return 0;\\n  if (n > 1) return 1;\\n  return n;\\n}\\n\",\"lineCount\":794},{\"path\":\"core/context-gate.ts\",\"content\":\"import type { ProjectMemory } from \\\"./project-memory.js\\\";\\nimport type { ChangeSet } from \\\"./change-set.js\\\";\\nimport type { PlanWave } from \\\"./multi-file-planner.js\\\";\\nimport type { Invariant } from \\\"./invariant-extractor.js\\\";\\n\\nexport interface GatedContext {\\n  relevantFiles: string[];\\n  recentTaskSummaries: string[];\\n  language: string;\\n  clusterFiles?: string[];\\n  landmines?: string[];\\n  safeApproaches?: string[];\\n  memoryNotes?: string[];\\n  suggestedNextSteps?: string[];\\n  strictVerification?: boolean;\\n  /**\\n   * Shared invariants the current wave must respect. Populated only\\n   * when gating is wave-aware (see gateContextForWave). Empty on\\n   * plain gateContext calls so single-file runs don't see a token\\n   * bloat they don't need.\\n   */\\n  waveInvariants?: WaveInvariantRef[];\\n  /**\\n   * Sibling files drawn from the same wave — not the whole plan.\\n   * Included only when gating is wave-aware AND the sibling is likely\\n   * relevant (filename overlap with the prompt, or shared invariant\\n   * name). Minimal-context discipline is enforced by\\n   * MAX_WAVE_SIBLINGS.\\n   */\\n  waveSiblings?: string[];\\n  /**\\n   * Ordered log of reasons for every item we injected. Every entry\\n   * maps 1:1 to an item in relevantFiles / waveInvariants /\\n   * waveSiblings. The Coordinator prints this so reviewers can audit\\n   * what the gate showed the worker and why.\\n   */\\n  inclusionLog?: string[];\\n}\\n\\nexport interface WaveInvariantRef {\\n  readonly name: string;\\n  readonly type: Invariant[\\\"type\\\"];\\n  readonly description: string;\\n}\\n\\nconst MAX_RELEVANT_FILES = 10;\\nconst MAX_RECENT_TASKS = 3;\\nconst MAX_WAVE_INVARIANTS = 12;\\nconst MAX_WAVE_SIBLINGS = 6;\\nconst MAX_ARCHITECTURAL_HUB_FILES = 10;\\n\\n/**\\n * Base context gate — used when there is no wave context yet (single-file\\n * runs, scouting, pre-charter memory lookups). Keeps a flat, minimal\\n * signature so the hot path stays cheap.\\n */\\nexport function gateContext(memory: ProjectMemory, prompt: string): GatedContext {\\n  const words = extractPromptWords(prompt);\\n\\n  const recentFiles = memory.recentFiles ?? [];\\n  const relevantFiles = words.length === 0\\n    ? []\\n    : recentFiles\\n        .filter(path => {\\n          const normalizedPath = path.toLowerCase();\\n          return words.some(word => normalizedPath.includes(word));\\n        })\\n        .sort()\\n        .slice(0, MAX_RELEVANT_FILES);\\n\\n  const recentTasks = memory.recentTasks ?? [];\\n  const recentTaskSummaries = recentTasks\\n    .slice(0, MAX_RECENT_TASKS)\\n    .map(task => (task.resultSummary ?? task.prompt).slice(0, 120));\\n\\n  return {\\n    relevantFiles,\\n    recentTaskSummaries,\\n    language: memory.language ?? \\\"unknown\\\",\\n  };\\n}\\n\\nexport interface WaveContextInputs {\\n  readonly memory: ProjectMemory;\\n  readonly prompt: string;\\n  readonly changeSet: ChangeSet;\\n  readonly wave: PlanWave;\\n  /**\\n   * Target files the current worker has been assigned. Used to pick\\n   * siblings — we never return the target file itself as a sibling,\\n   * and we only pick from the wave's own file list.\\n   */\\n  readonly targetFiles: readonly string[];\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Wave-aware gate — used by the Coordinator when dispatching a builder\\n * inside a multi-file plan. Returns a GatedContext with:\\n *\\n *   - relevantFiles         — same base heuristic as gateContext\\n *   - waveInvariants        — only invariants whose `files` overlap\\n *                             the current wave's file set. Invariants\\n *                             that apply to later waves are deliberately\\n *                             withheld to preserve minimal-context\\n *                             discipline.\\n *   - waveSiblings          — files in the same wave, ranked by filename\\n *                             overlap with the prompt or shared\\n *                             invariant names. Capped at\\n *                             MAX_WAVE_SIBLINGS.\\n *   - inclusionLog          — one line per injected item explaining\\n *                             the reason (for Coordinator logging).\\n */\\nexport function gateContextForWave(inputs: WaveContextInputs): GatedContext {\\n  const { memory, prompt, changeSet, wave, targetFiles } = inputs;\\n  const base = gateContext(memory, prompt);\\n  const log: string[] = [];\\n\\n  for (const file of base.relevantFiles) {\\n    log.push(`relevant: ${file} — prompt word match on project memory`);\\n  }\\n\\n  const waveFiles = new Set(wave.files);\\n  const waveInvariants: WaveInvariantRef[] = [];\\n  for (const invariant of changeSet.invariants) {\\n    const touchesWave = invariant.files.some((f) => waveFiles.has(f));\\n    if (!touchesWave) continue;\\n\\n    waveInvariants.push({\\n      name: invariant.name,\\n      type: invariant.type,\\n      description: invariant.description,\\n    });\\n    log.push(\\n      `invariant: ${invariant.type}:${invariant.name} — touches ${invariant.files.filter((f) => waveFiles.has(f)).length} file(s) in wave ${wave.id}`,\\n    );\\n    if (waveInvariants.length >= MAX_WAVE_INVARIANTS) break;\\n  }\\n\\n  const words = extractPromptWords(prompt);\\n  const targetSet = new Set(targetFiles);\\n  const siblingCandidates = wave.files.filter((f) => !targetSet.has(f));\\n\\n  const rankedSiblings = siblingCandidates\\n    .map((file) => {\\n      const normalized = file.toLowerCase();\\n      const promptMatch = words.some((w) => normalized.includes(w));\\n      const sharesInvariant = waveInvariants.some((inv) =>\\n        changeSet.invariants.some(\\n          (i) => i.name === inv.name && i.files.includes(file),\\n        ),\\n      );\\n      return { file, promptMatch, sharesInvariant };\\n    })\\n    .filter((entry) => entry.promptMatch || entry.sharesInvariant)\\n    .slice(0, MAX_WAVE_SIBLINGS);\\n\\n  for (const entry of rankedSiblings) {\\n    const reasons: string[] = [];\\n    if (entry.promptMatch) reasons.push(\\\"prompt word match\\\");\\n    if (entry.sharesInvariant) reasons.push(\\\"shared invariant\\\");\\n    log.push(`sibling: ${entry.file} — ${reasons.join(\\\", \\\")}`);\\n  }\\n\\n  return {\\n    ...base,\\n    waveInvariants,\\n    waveSiblings: rankedSiblings.map((entry) => entry.file),\\n    inclusionLog: log,\\n  };\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Architectural-mode gate — triggered when scopeClassification.type === 'architectural'.\\n * Reads the repo index to find the top 10 most-connected files (files imported\\n * by the most other files), includes those as context regardless of prompt\\n * relevance, and injects a repo-wide summary at the top of every worker prompt.\\n */\\nexport function gateContextForArchitectural(\\n  memory: ProjectMemory,\\n  prompt: string,\\n  repoIndex: { file: string; importedByCount: number }[],\\n): GatedContext {\\n  const base = gateContext(memory, prompt);\\n  const log: string[] = [];\\n\\n  for (const file of base.relevantFiles) {\\n    log.push(`relevant: ${file} — prompt word match on project memory`);\\n  }\\n\\n  // Find top N most-connected hub files from the repo index\\n  const sorted = [...repoIndex]\\n    .sort((a, b) => b.importedByCount - a.importedByCount)\\n    .slice(0, MAX_ARCHITECTURAL_HUB_FILES);\\n\\n  const hubFiles = sorted.map((entry) => entry.file);\\n  for (const entry of sorted) {\\n    log.push(\\n      `hub: ${entry.file} — imported by ${entry.importedByCount} file(s) (architectural mode)`,\\n    );\\n  }\\n\\n  // Merge hub files into relevant files, deduped\\n  const allRelevant = uniqueStrings([...base.relevantFiles, ...hubFiles]);\\n\\n  const architecturalNote =\\n    `This is an architectural change. Key hub files: ${hubFiles.join(\\\", \\\")}. ` +\\n    `Proceed with awareness of downstream impact.`;\\n\\n  return {\\n    ...base,\\n    relevantFiles: allRelevant,\\n    memoryNotes: [...(base.memoryNotes ?? []), architecturalNote],\\n    inclusionLog: log,\\n  };\\n}\\n\\nexport function mergeGatedContext(base: GatedContext, overlay?: Partial<GatedContext>): GatedContext {\\n  if (!overlay) return base;\\n  return {\\n    relevantFiles: uniqueStrings([...base.relevantFiles, ...(overlay.relevantFiles ?? [])]),\\n    recentTaskSummaries: uniqueStrings([...base.recentTaskSummaries, ...(overlay.recentTaskSummaries ?? [])]).slice(0, MAX_RECENT_TASKS + 3),\\n    language: overlay.language ?? base.language,\\n    ...(mergeOptionalArrays(base.clusterFiles, overlay.clusterFiles, \\\"clusterFiles\\\")),\\n    ...(mergeOptionalArrays(base.landmines, overlay.landmines, \\\"landmines\\\")),\\n    ...(mergeOptionalArrays(base.safeApproaches, overlay.safeApproaches, \\\"safeApproaches\\\")),\\n    ...(mergeOptionalArrays(base.memoryNotes, overlay.memoryNotes, \\\"memoryNotes\\\")),\\n    ...(mergeOptionalArrays(base.suggestedNextSteps, overlay.suggestedNextSteps, \\\"suggestedNextSteps\\\")),\\n    ...(mergeOptionalArrays(base.waveInvariants, overlay.waveInvariants, \\\"waveInvariants\\\")),\\n    ...(mergeOptionalArrays(base.waveSiblings, overlay.waveSiblings, \\\"waveSiblings\\\")),\\n    ...(mergeOptionalArrays(base.inclusionLog, overlay.inclusionLog, \\\"inclusionLog\\\")),\\n    ...(overlay.strictVerification !== undefined ? { strictVerification: overlay.strictVerification } : base.strictVerification !== undefined ? { strictVerification: base.strictVerification } : {}),\\n  };\\n}\\n\\nfunction extractPromptWords(prompt: string): string[] {\\n  return Array.from(\\n    new Set(\\n      prompt\\n        .toLowerCase()\\n        .split(/\\\\s+/)\\n        .map(word => word.replace(/[^a-z0-9_-]/g, \\\"\\\"))\\n        .filter(word => word.length >= 4)\\n    )\\n  );\\n}\\n\\nfunction uniqueStrings(values: readonly string[]): string[] {\\n  return Array.from(new Set(values.filter((value): value is string => typeof value === \\\"string\\\" && value.length > 0)));\\n}\\n\\nfunction mergeOptionalArrays<T>(\\n  base: readonly T[] | undefined,\\n  overlay: readonly T[] | undefined,\\n  key: \\\"clusterFiles\\\" | \\\"landmines\\\" | \\\"safeApproaches\\\" | \\\"memoryNotes\\\" | \\\"suggestedNextSteps\\\" | \\\"waveInvariants\\\" | \\\"waveSiblings\\\" | \\\"inclusionLog\\\",\\n): Partial<GatedContext> {\\n  const values = [...(base ?? []), ...(overlay ?? [])];\\n  if (values.length === 0) return {};\\n  return {\\n    [key]: Array.from(new Set(values)),\\n  } as Partial<GatedContext>;\\n}\\n\",\"lineCount\":262},{\"path\":\"core/model-invoker.ts\",\"content\":\"/**\\n * ModelInvoker — Unified model caller for all Aedis providers.\\n *\\n * Reads API keys from process.env. Logs every call.\\n *\\n * Providers:\\n *   - ollama: POST http://localhost:11434/api/chat\\n *   - modelstudio: OpenAI-compatible, MODELSTUDIO_BASE_URL + MODELSTUDIO_API_KEY\\n *   - openrouter: https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions + OPENROUTER_API_KEY\\n *   - anthropic: https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages + ANTHROPIC_API_KEY\\n *   - openai: https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions + OPENAI_API_KEY\\n *   - minimax: MiniMax chat completions + MINIMAX_API_KEY\\n *   - zai: OpenAI-compatible, ZAI_BASE_URL + ZAI_API_KEY\\n *   - glm-5.1-openrouter: GLM-5.1 via OpenRouter (z-ai/glm-5.1) + OPENROUTER_API_KEY\\n *   - glm-5.1-direct: GLM-5.1 via ZAI direct (open.bigmodel.cn) + ZAI_API_KEY\\n *   - portum: OpenAI-compatible, http://localhost:18797/v1/chat/completions, no API key\\n *   - local: mock response, zero cost\\n *\\n * Fallback chain:\\n *   invokeModelWithFallback() walks a chain of InvokeConfigs, trying each\\n *   in order. If a provider times out, it is added to the run's blacklist\\n *   and never retried within the same run. Other errors fall through to\\n *   the next chain entry without blacklisting (e.g. transient HTTP errors\\n *   on a different provider may still be worth a future attempt).\\n *\\n *   After the caller-provided chain is exhausted, a final last-resort\\n *   attempt is made against portum/qwen3.6-plus. Portum is the local\\n *   OpenAI-compatible gateway at localhost:18797 — it serves as a universal\\n *   safety net for every worker without each worker needing to know about\\n *   it. The last-resort attempt is skipped if portum was already in the\\n *   caller's chain or if it was blacklisted (timed out) earlier in the run.\\n *\\n * Default timeout: 5 minutes (300_000 ms). Builders sending large prompts\\n * to slower providers were tripping the previous 2-minute cap. Workers\\n * that need a tighter bound can pass an explicit timeoutMs.\\n */\\n\\n// ─── Types ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nexport type Provider =\\n  | \\\"ollama\\\"\\n  | \\\"modelstudio\\\"\\n  | \\\"openrouter\\\"\\n  | \\\"anthropic\\\"\\n  | \\\"openai\\\"\\n  | \\\"minimax\\\"\\n  | \\\"zai\\\"\\n  | \\\"glm-5.1-openrouter\\\"\\n  | \\\"glm-5.1-direct\\\"\\n  | \\\"portum\\\"\\n  | \\\"local\\\";\\n\\nexport interface InvokeConfig {\\n  provider: Provider;\\n  model: string;\\n  prompt: string;\\n  systemPrompt?: string;\\n  maxTokens?: number;\\n  /** Run ID threaded through to the call log for scoped cost aggregation. */\\n  runId?: string;\\n}\\n\\nexport interface InvokeResult {\\n  text: string;\\n  tokensIn: number;\\n  tokensOut: number;\\n  costUsd: number;\\n}\\n\\nexport interface FallbackInvokeResult extends InvokeResult {\\n  /** The provider that actually returned the result. */\\n  readonly usedProvider: Provider;\\n  /** The model that actually returned the result. */\\n  readonly usedModel: string;\\n  /** Every provider that was attempted, in order, including the one that succeeded. */\\n  readonly attemptedProviders: readonly Provider[];\\n  /** Whether at least one provider was skipped due to a prior timeout in the same run. */\\n  readonly skippedDueToBlacklist: boolean;\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Per-run state for fallback invocation. Holds the set of providers that\\n * have timed out within the current run so they are never retried.\\n *\\n * Callers (Coordinator, BuilderWorker) own the lifecycle: create one per\\n * run, pass it to every invokeModelWithFallback call within that run,\\n * discard it when the run ends.\\n */\\nexport interface RunInvocationContext {\\n  readonly timedOutProviders: Set<Provider>;\\n  /** Number of confidence-based escalations performed in this run. */\\n  escalationCount: number;\\n}\\n\\nexport function createRunInvocationContext(): RunInvocationContext {\\n  return { timedOutProviders: new Set<Provider>(), escalationCount: 0 };\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Last-resort fallback ────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/**\\n * Universal last-resort entry appended after every caller-provided chain.\\n * Portum is the local OpenAI-compatible gateway running on port 18797 and\\n * has no API-key requirement, so it can be reached unconditionally as long\\n * as the local service is up.\\n */\\nconst PORTUM_LAST_RESORT: { provider: Provider; model: string } = {\\n  provider: \\\"portum\\\",\\n  model: \\\"qwen3.6-plus\\\",\\n};\\n\\n// ─── Cost Table (per 1K tokens) ──────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nconst COST_PER_1K: Record<string, { input: number; output: number }> = {\\n  // Ollama — local, free\\n  \\\"local\\\":            { input: 0,      output: 0      },\\n  \\\"qwen3.5:4b\\\":      { input: 0,      output: 0      },\\n  \\\"qwen3.5:9b\\\":      { input: 0,      output: 0      },\\n  // ModelStudio\\n  \\\"qwen3.6-plus\\\":    { input: 0.0008, output: 0.002  },\\n  \\\"glm-4\\\":           { input: 0.001,  output: 0.002  },\\n  // OpenRouter\\n  \\\"xiaomi/mimo-v2-pro\\\": { input: 0.0005, output: 0.0015 },\\n  // Anthropic\\n  \\\"claude-opus-4-6\\\":   { input: 0.015,  output: 0.075  },\\n  \\\"claude-sonnet-4-6\\\": { input: 0.003,  output: 0.015  },\\n  // OpenAI\\n  \\\"gpt-4o\\\":          { input: 0.0025, output: 0.01   },\\n  \\\"gpt-5.4\\\":         { input: 0.005,  output: 0.015  },\\n  // MiniMax\\n  \\\"minimax-coding\\\":  { input: 0.0004, output: 0.0016 },\\n  // ZAI\\n  \\\"glm-5.1\\\":        { input: 0.002,  output: 0.006  },\\n  // GLM-5.1 via OpenRouter ($0.95/M in, $3.15/M out)\\n  \\\"z-ai/glm-5.1\\\":   { input: 0.00095, output: 0.00315 },\\n};\\n\\nfunction estimateCost(model: string, tokensIn: number, tokensOut: number): number {\\n  const rates = COST_PER_1K[model] ?? { input: 0.001, output: 0.003 };\\n  return Number(((tokensIn / 1000) * rates.input + (tokensOut / 1000) * rates.output).toFixed(6));\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Call Log ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nexport interface CallLogEntry {\\n  timestamp: string;\\n  provider: Provider;\\n  model: string;\\n  tokensIn: number;\\n  tokensOut: number;\\n  costUsd: number;\\n  durationMs: number;\\n  error?: string;\\n  /** Run ID for scoped cost aggregation. */\\n  runId?: string;\\n}\\n\\nconst callLog: CallLogEntry[] = [];\\n\\nexport function getCallLog(): readonly CallLogEntry[] {\\n  return callLog;\\n}\\n\\nexport function getCallLogSummary(): { totalCalls: number; totalCostUsd: number; totalTokensIn: number; totalTokensOut: number } {\\n  return {\\n    totalCalls: callLog.length,\\n    totalCostUsd: Number(callLog.reduce((sum, e) => sum + e.costUsd, 0).toFixed(6)),\\n    totalTokensIn: callLog.reduce((sum, e) => sum + e.tokensIn, 0),\\n    totalTokensOut: callLog.reduce((sum, e) => sum + e.tokensOut, 0),\\n  };\\n}\\n\\nfunction logCall(entry: CallLogEntry): void {\\n  callLog.push(entry);\\n  // Keep last 500 entries\\n  if (callLog.length > 500) callLog.shift();\\n  console.log(\\n    `[model-invoker] ${entry.provider}/${entry.model} — ${entry.tokensIn}in/${entry.tokensOut}out — $${entry.costUsd.toFixed(6)} — ${entry.durationMs}ms${entry.error ? ` — ERROR: ${entry.error}` : \\\"\\\"}`\\n  );\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Main Entry Point ────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nexport async function invokeModel(config: InvokeConfig): Promise<InvokeResult> {\\n  const { provider, model, prompt, systemPrompt, maxTokens } = config;\\n  const startMs = Date.now();\\n\\n  try {\\n    let result: InvokeResult;\\n\\n    switch (provider) {\\n      case \\\"local\\\":\\n        result = invokeLocal(prompt);\\n        break;\\n      case \\\"ollama\\\":\\n        result = await invokeOllama(model, prompt, systemPrompt, maxTokens);\\n        break;\\n      case \\\"modelstudio\\\":\\n        result = await invokeOpenAICompatible(\\n          process.env.MODELSTUDIO_BASE_URL ?? \\\"https://dashscope.aliyuncs.com/compatible-mode/v1\\\",\\n          requireEnv(\\\"MODELSTUDIO_API_KEY\\\"),\\n          model, prompt, systemPrompt, maxTokens,\\n        );\\n        break;\\n      case \\\"openrouter\\\":\\n        result = await invokeOpenAICompatible(\\n          \\\"https://openrouter.ai/api/v1\\\",\\n          requireEnv(\\\"OPENROUTER_API_KEY\\\"),\\n          model, prompt, systemPrompt, maxTokens,\\n        );\\n        break;\\n      case \\\"anthropic\\\":\\n        result = await invokeAnthropic(\\n          requireEnv(\\\"ANTHROPIC_API_KEY\\\"),\\n          model, prompt, systemPrompt, maxTokens,\\n        );\\n        break;\\n      case \\\"openai\\\":\\n        result = await invokeOpenAICompatible(\\n          process.env.OPENAI_BASE_URL ?? \\\"https://api.openai.com/v1\\\",\\n          requireEnv(\\\"OPENAI_API_KEY\\\"),\\n          model, prompt, systemPrompt, maxTokens,\\n        );\\n        break;\\n      case \\\"minimax\\\":\\n        result = await invokeOpenAICompatible(\\n          process.env.MINIMAX_BASE_URL ?? \\\"https://api.minimax.chat/v1\\\",\\n          requireEnv(\\\"MINIMAX_API_KEY\\\"),\\n          model, prompt, systemPrompt, maxTokens,\\n        );\\n        break;\\n      case \\\"zai\\\":\\n        result = await invokeOpenAICompatible(\\n          process.env.ZAI_BASE_URL ?? \\\"https://api.z.ai/api/paas/v4/\\\",\\n          requireEnv(\\\"ZAI_API_KEY\\\"),\\n          model, prompt, systemPrompt, maxTokens,\\n        );\\n        break;\\n      case \\\"glm-5.1-openrouter\\\":\\n        result = await invokeOpenAICompatible(\\n          \\\"https://openrouter.ai/api/v1\\\",\\n          requireEnv(\\\"OPENROUTER_API_KEY\\\"),\\n          \\\"z-ai/glm-5.1\\\", prompt, systemPrompt, maxTokens,\\n        );\\n        break;\\n      case \\\"glm-5.1-direct\\\":\\n        result = await invokeOpenAICompatible(\\n          \\\"https://open.bigmodel.cn/api/paas/v4\\\",\\n          requireEnv(\\\"ZAI_API_KEY\\\"),\\n          \\\"glm-5.1\\\", prompt, systemPrompt, maxTokens,\\n        );\\n        break;\\n      case \\\"portum\\\":\\n        result = await invokeOpenAICompatible(\\n          \\\"http://localhost:18797/v1\\\",\\n          undefined,\\n          model, prompt, systemPrompt, maxTokens,\\n        );\\n        break;\\n      default:\\n        throw new InvokerError(`Unknown provider \\\"${provider}\\\"`, \\\"config\\\");\\n    }\\n\\n    logCall({\\n      timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),\\n      provider, model,\\n      tokensIn: result.tokensIn,\\n      tokensOut: result.tokensOut,\\n      costUsd: result.costUsd,\\n      durationMs: Date.now() - startMs,\\n      ...(config.runId ? { runId: config.runId } : {}),\\n    });\\n\\n    return result;\\n  } catch (err) {\\n    const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);\\n    logCall({\\n      timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),\\n      provider, model,\\n      tokensIn: 0, tokensOut: 0, costUsd: 0,\\n      durationMs: Date.now() - startMs,\\n      error: msg,\\n      ...(config.runId ? { runId: config.runId } : {}),\\n    });\\n    throw err;\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Invoke a model with a fallback chain.\\n *\\n * Walks `chain` in order. For each entry:\\n *   - If the provider is in `runContext.timedOutProviders`, skip it and log.\\n *   - Otherwise call invokeModel(). On success, return immediately.\\n *   - On InvokerError of kind \\\"timeout\\\": add provider to blacklist, continue.\\n *   - On any other error: continue to next entry without blacklisting.\\n *\\n * After the caller-provided chain is fully exhausted, a final last-resort\\n * attempt is made against portum/qwen3.6-plus (the local OpenAI-compatible\\n * gateway). The last-resort is skipped if portum was already in the chain\\n * (no point in double-attempting) or if portum is in the blacklist\\n * (timed out earlier in this run). If every entry — including the\\n * last-resort — fails, throws an aggregated InvokerError.\\n *\\n * The runContext is mutated in place — callers can pass the same context\\n * across multiple invokeModelWithFallback calls within a single run, and\\n * the timeout blacklist accumulates across the whole run.\\n */\\nexport async function invokeModelWithFallback(\\n  chain: readonly InvokeConfig[],\\n  runContext?: RunInvocationContext,\\n): Promise<FallbackInvokeResult> {\\n  if (chain.length === 0) {\\n    throw new InvokerError(\\\"invokeModelWithFallback: chain is empty\\\", \\\"config\\\");\\n  }\\n\\n  const ctx = runContext ?? createRunInvocationContext();\\n  const attemptedProviders: Provider[] = [];\\n  const errors: string[] = [];\\n  let skippedDueToBlacklist = false;\\n\\n  for (const cfg of chain) {\\n    if (ctx.timedOutProviders.has(cfg.provider)) {\\n      console.warn(\\n        `[model-invoker] fallback: skipping ${cfg.provider}/${cfg.model} — provider is blacklisted (timed out earlier in this run)`\\n      );\\n      skippedDueToBlacklist = true;\\n      continue;\\n    }\\n\\n    attemptedProviders.push(cfg.provider);\\n    console.log(`[model-invoker] fallback: attempting ${cfg.provider}/${cfg.model}`);\\n\\n    try {\\n      const result = await invokeModel(cfg);\\n      console.log(`[model-invoker] fallback: ${cfg.provider}/${cfg.model} succeeded`);\\n      return {\\n        ...result,\\n        usedProvider: cfg.provider,\\n        usedModel: cfg.model,\\n        attemptedProviders,\\n        skippedDueToBlacklist,\\n      };\\n    } catch (err) {\\n      const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);\\n      const kind = err instanceof InvokerError ? err.kind : \\\"unknown\\\";\\n      errors.push(`${cfg.provider}/${cfg.model} (${kind}): ${msg}`);\\n\\n      if (kind === \\\"timeout\\\") {\\n        console.warn(\\n          `[model-invoker] fallback: ${cfg.provider}/${cfg.model} TIMED OUT — blacklisting provider for the rest of this run`\\n        );\\n        ctx.timedOutProviders.add(cfg.provider);\\n      } else {\\n        console.warn(\\n          `[model-invoker] fallback: ${cfg.provider}/${cfg.model} failed (${kind}) — trying next in chain`\\n        );\\n      }\\n      // Continue to next chain entry\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  // Snapshot how many of the original chain entries were tried vs. skipped\\n  // BEFORE we add Portum to attemptedProviders. The error message below\\n  // reports both numbers, and we don't want Portum's last-resort entry to\\n  // skew the chain-skip count.\\n  const chainEntriesAttempted = attemptedProviders.length;\\n  const chainEntriesSkipped = chain.length - chainEntriesAttempted;\\n\\n  // ─── Last resort: Portum ──────────────────────────────────────────\\n  // Portum is the local OpenAI-compatible gateway at localhost:18797 and\\n  // requires no API key, so it can be reached unconditionally as long as\\n  // the local service is up. We try it once after the caller's chain is\\n  // exhausted — but only if Portum wasn't already in the chain (avoid\\n  // double-attempting) and isn't blacklisted from a prior timeout.\\n  const portumInChain = chain.some((cfg) => cfg.provider === PORTUM_LAST_RESORT.provider);\\n  const portumBlacklisted = ctx.timedOutProviders.has(PORTUM_LAST_RESORT.provider);\\n\\n  if (!portumInChain && !portumBlacklisted) {\\n    // Inherit prompt/systemPrompt/maxTokens from the most recent chain\\n    // entry — in current usage every chain entry shares the same prompt,\\n    // and the last entry is the most recently constructed so it tends to\\n    // reflect any per-run adjustments.\\n    const template = chain[chain.length - 1]!;\\n    const portumCfg: InvokeConfig = {\\n      provider: PORTUM_LAST_RESORT.provider,\\n      model: PORTUM_LAST_RESORT.model,\\n      prompt: template.prompt,\\n      systemPrompt: template.systemPrompt,\\n      maxTokens: template.maxTokens,\\n    };\\n\\n    attemptedProviders.push(PORTUM_LAST_RESORT.provider);\\n    console.log(\\n      `[model-invoker] fallback: chain exhausted — last-resort attempt ${PORTUM_LAST_RESORT.provider}/${PORTUM_LAST_RESORT.model}`\\n    );\\n\\n    try {\\n      const result = await invokeModel(portumCfg);\\n      console.log(\\n        `[model-invoker] fallback: ${PORTUM_LAST_RESORT.provider}/${PORTUM_LAST_RESORT.model} succeeded as last resort`\\n      );\\n      return {\\n        ...result,\\n        usedProvider: PORTUM_LAST_RESORT.provider,\\n        usedModel: PORTUM_LAST_RESORT.model,\\n        attemptedProviders,\\n        skippedDueToBlacklist,\\n      };\\n    } catch (err) {\\n      const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);\\n      const kind = err instanceof InvokerError ? err.kind : \\\"unknown\\\";\\n      errors.push(`${PORTUM_LAST_RESORT.provider}/${PORTUM_LAST_RESORT.model} (${kind}): ${msg}`);\\n      if (kind === \\\"timeout\\\") {\\n        ctx.timedOutProviders.add(PORTUM_LAST_RESORT.provider);\\n      }\\n      console.warn(\\n        `[model-invoker] fallback: portum last-resort failed (${kind}) — giving up`\\n      );\\n    }\\n  } else if (portumInChain) {\\n    console.warn(\\n      \\\"[model-invoker] fallback: chain exhausted — portum was already in caller chain, skipping last-resort\\\"\\n    );\\n  } else {\\n    // portum is blacklisted from an earlier timeout\\n    console.warn(\\n      \\\"[model-invoker] fallback: chain exhausted — portum is blacklisted (timed out earlier in this run), skipping last-resort\\\"\\n    );\\n    skippedDueToBlacklist = true;\\n  }\\n\\n  throw new InvokerError(\\n    `All fallback providers failed (${chainEntriesAttempted} chain entries attempted, ${chainEntriesSkipped} skipped via blacklist, plus portum last-resort): ${errors.join(\\\" | \\\")}`,\\n    \\\"unknown\\\",\\n  );\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Local (Mock) ────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nfunction invokeLocal(prompt: string): InvokeResult {\\n  return {\\n    text: `[local] Acknowledged (${prompt.length} chars). No model call.`,\\n    tokensIn: Math.ceil(prompt.length / 4),\\n    tokensOut: 16,\\n    costUsd: 0,\\n  };\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Ollama ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nasync function invokeOllama(\\n  model: string,\\n  prompt: string,\\n  systemPrompt?: string,\\n  _maxTokens?: number,\\n): Promise<InvokeResult> {\\n  const base = process.env.OLLAMA_BASE_URL ?? \\\"http://localhost:11434\\\";\\n\\n  const messages: Array<{ role: string; content: string }> = [];\\n  if (systemPrompt) messages.push({ role: \\\"system\\\", content: systemPrompt });\\n  messages.push({ role: \\\"user\\\", content: prompt });\\n\\n  const res = await fetchWithTimeout(`${base}/api/chat`, {\\n    method: \\\"POST\\\",\\n    headers: { \\\"Content-Type\\\": \\\"application/json\\\" },\\n    body: JSON.stringify({ model, messages, stream: false }),\\n  });\\n\\n  if (!res.ok) {\\n    throw new InvokerError(\\n      `Ollama ${model}: ${res.status} ${await res.text().catch(() => \\\"\\\")}`,\\n      \\\"http\\\",\\n    );\\n  }\\n\\n  const data = await res.json() as any;\\n  const text = data.message?.content ?? \\\"\\\";\\n  const tokensIn = data.prompt_eval_count ?? Math.ceil(prompt.length / 4);\\n  const tokensOut = data.eval_count ?? Math.ceil(text.length / 4);\\n\\n  return { text, tokensIn, tokensOut, costUsd: estimateCost(model, tokensIn, tokensOut) };\\n}\\n\\n// ─── OpenAI-Compatible (ModelStudio, OpenRouter, OpenAI, MiniMax, ZAI, Portum) ─\\n\\nasync function invokeOpenAICompatible(\\n  baseUrl: string,\\n  apiKey: string | undefined,\\n  model: string,\\n  prompt: string,\\n  systemPrompt?: string,\\n  maxTokens?: number,\\n): Promise<InvokeResult> {\\n  const messages: Array<{ role: string; content: string }> = [];\\n  if (systemPrompt) messages.push({ role: \\\"system\\\", content: systemPrompt });\\n  messages.push({ role: \\\"user\\\", content: prompt });\\n\\n  const headers: Record<string, string> = {\\n    \\\"Content-Type\\\": \\\"application/json\\\",\\n  };\\n\\n  if (apiKey) {\\n    headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${apiKey}`;\\n  }\\n\\n  const res = await fetchWithTimeout(`${baseUrl}/chat/completions`, {\\n    method: \\\"POST\\\",\\n    headers,\\n    body: JSON.stringify({\\n      model,\\n      messages,\\n      max_tokens: maxTokens ?? 4096,\\n      temperature: 0.2,\\n    }),\\n  });\\n\\n  if (!res.ok) {\\n    const errBody = await res.text().catch(() => \\\"\\\");\\n    throw new InvokerError(\\n      `${model} via ${baseUrl}: ${res.status} ${res.statusText}\\\\n${errBody}`,\\n      \\\"http\\\",\\n    );\\n  }\\n\\n  const data = await res.json() as any;\\n  const text = data.choices?.[0]?.message?.content ?? \\\"\\\";\\n  const usage = data.usage ?? {};\\n  const tokensIn = usage.prompt_tokens ?? Math.ceil(prompt.length / 4);\\n  const tokensOut = usage.completion_tokens ?? Math.ceil(text.length / 4);\\n\\n  return { text, tokensIn, tokensOut, costUsd: estimateCost(model, tokensIn, tokensOut) };\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Anthropic ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nasync function invokeAnthropic(\\n  apiKey: string,\\n  model: string,\\n  prompt: string,\\n  systemPrompt?: string,\\n  maxTokens?: number,\\n): Promise<InvokeResult> {\\n  const body: Record<string, unknown> = {\\n    model,\\n    max_tokens: maxTokens ?? 4096,\\n    messages: [{ role: \\\"user\\\", content: prompt }],\\n  };\\n  if (systemPrompt) body.system = systemPrompt;\\n\\n  const res = await fetchWithTimeout(\\\"https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages\\\", {\\n    method: \\\"POST\\\",\\n    headers: {\\n      \\\"Content-Type\\\": \\\"application/json\\\",\\n      \\\"x-api-key\\\": apiKey,\\n      \\\"anthropic-version\\\": \\\"2023-06-01\\\",\\n    },\\n    body: JSON.stringify(body),\\n  });\\n\\n  if (!res.ok) {\\n    const errBody = await res.text().catch(() => \\\"\\\");\\n    throw new InvokerError(\\n      `Anthropic ${model}: ${res.status} ${res.statusText}\\\\n${errBody}`,\\n      \\\"http\\\",\\n    );\\n  }\\n\\n  const data = await res.json() as any;\\n  const text = (data.content ?? [])\\n    .filter((block: any) => block.type === \\\"text\\\")\\n    .map((block: any) => block.text)\\n    .join(\\\"\\\");\\n  const tokensIn = data.usage?.input_tokens ?? Math.ceil(prompt.length / 4);\\n  const tokensOut = data.usage?.output_tokens ?? Math.ceil(text.length / 4);\\n\\n  return { text, tokensIn, tokensOut, costUsd: estimateCost(model, tokensIn, tokensOut) };\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nfunction requireEnv(name: string): string {\\n  const val = process.env[name];\\n  if (!val) throw new InvokerError(`Missing environment variable: ${name}`, \\\"config\\\");\\n  return val;\\n}\\n\\nasync function fetchWithTimeout(\\n  url: string,\\n  init: RequestInit,\\n  timeoutMs: number = 300_000,\\n): Promise<Response> {\\n  const controller = new AbortController();\\n  const timer = setTimeout(() => controller.abort(), timeoutMs);\\n  try {\\n    return await fetch(url, { ...init, signal: controller.signal });\\n  } catch (err: any) {\\n    if (err.name === \\\"AbortError\\\") {\\n      throw new InvokerError(`Request to ${url} timed out after ${timeoutMs}ms`, \\\"timeout\\\");\\n    }\\n    throw new InvokerError(`Network error calling ${url}: ${err.message ?? err}`, \\\"network\\\");\\n  } finally {\\n    clearTimeout(timer);\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Confidence-Based Escalation ────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nexport interface EscalationResult {\\n  /** Whether an escalation was performed. */\\n  readonly escalated: boolean;\\n  /** The result from the escalation attempt (if escalated). */\\n  readonly result: InvokeResult | null;\\n  /** The provider used for escalation. */\\n  readonly escalationProvider: Provider | null;\\n  /** The model used for escalation. */\\n  readonly escalationModel: string | null;\\n  /** Reason for escalation or why it was skipped. */\\n  readonly reason: string;\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Check a builder result's confidence and escalate to a better model if\\n * confidence is below the threshold. Capped at 1 escalation per run to\\n * prevent cost runaway.\\n *\\n * @param confidence  The confidence score from the builder result (0-1).\\n * @param config      The original InvokeConfig (prompt, systemPrompt, etc.)\\n *                    to retry with the escalated model.\\n * @param runContext  Per-run invocation context tracking escalation count.\\n * @param threshold   Confidence threshold below which to escalate (default 0.6).\\n * @returns           EscalationResult with the retry outcome.\\n */\\nexport async function escalateOnLowConfidence(\\n  confidence: number,\\n  config: InvokeConfig,\\n  runContext: RunInvocationContext,\\n  threshold: number = 0.6,\\n): Promise<EscalationResult> {\\n  if (confidence >= threshold) {\\n    return {\\n      escalated: false,\\n      result: null,\\n      escalationProvider: null,\\n      escalationModel: null,\\n      reason: `confidence ${(confidence * 100).toFixed(0)}% >= ${(threshold * 100).toFixed(0)}% threshold — no escalation needed`,\\n    };\\n  }\\n\\n  if (runContext.escalationCount >= 1) {\\n    console.log(\\n      `[model-invoker] low confidence (${(confidence * 100).toFixed(0)}%) but escalation cap reached (${runContext.escalationCount}/1) — skipping retry`,\\n    );\\n    return {\\n      escalated: false,\\n      result: null,\\n      escalationProvider: null,\\n      escalationModel: null,\\n      reason: `confidence ${(confidence * 100).toFixed(0)}% below threshold but escalation cap (1 per run) already reached`,\\n    };\\n  }\\n\\n  const escalationProvider: Provider = \\\"anthropic\\\";\\n  const escalationModel = \\\"claude-sonnet-4-6\\\";\\n\\n  console.log(\\n    `[model-invoker] low confidence (${(confidence * 100).toFixed(0)}%) — escalating to ${escalationProvider}/${escalationModel} for retry`,\\n  );\\n\\n  runContext.escalationCount += 1;\\n\\n  try {\\n    const result = await invokeModel({\\n      ...config,\\n      provider: escalationProvider,\\n      model: escalationModel,\\n    });\\n\\n    console.log(\\n      `[model-invoker] escalation to ${escalationProvider}/${escalationModel} succeeded — ` +\\n      `${result.tokensIn}in/${result.tokensOut}out $${result.costUsd.toFixed(6)}`,\\n    );\\n\\n    return {\\n      escalated: true,\\n      result,\\n      escalationProvider,\\n      escalationModel,\\n      reason: `confidence ${(confidence * 100).toFixed(0)}% below ${(threshold * 100).toFixed(0)}% threshold — escalated to ${escalationModel}`,\\n    };\\n  } catch (err) {\\n    const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);\\n    console.error(\\n      `[model-invoker] escalation to ${escalationProvider}/${escalationModel} failed: ${msg}`,\\n    );\\n    return {\\n      escalated: true,\\n      result: null,\\n      escalationProvider,\\n      escalationModel,\\n      reason: `escalation attempted but failed: ${msg}`,\\n    };\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Errors ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nexport type InvokerErrorKind = \\\"timeout\\\" | \\\"http\\\" | \\\"network\\\" | \\\"config\\\" | \\\"unknown\\\";\\n\\nexport class InvokerError extends Error {\\n  readonly kind: InvokerErrorKind;\\n  constructor(message: string, kind: InvokerErrorKind = \\\"unknown\\\") {\\n    super(message);\\n    this.name = \\\"InvokerError\\\";\\n    this.kind = kind;\\n  }\\n}\\n\",\"lineCount\":718},{\"path\":\"server/routes/tasks.ts\",\"content\":\"/**\\n * Task routes — Submit, query, and cancel build tasks.\\n *\\n * POST /tasks       — Submit a new task, returns { task_id, run_id }\\n * GET  /tasks/:id   — Status + current run state\\n * GET  /tasks/:id/receipts — Full receipt bundle\\n * POST /tasks/:id/cancel — Cancel a running task\\n * DELETE /tasks/:id — Cancel a running task\\n */\\n\\nimport { randomUUID } from \\\"crypto\\\";\\nimport { existsSync } from \\\"fs\\\";\\nimport type { FastifyPluginAsync, FastifyRequest, FastifyReply } from \\\"fastify\\\";\\nimport { askLoqui } from \\\"../../core/loqui.js\\\";\\nimport { routeLoquiInput, type LoquiRouteDecision } from \\\"../../core/loqui-router.js\\\";\\nimport type { LoquiIntentContext } from \\\"../../core/loqui-intent.js\\\";\\nimport { generateDryRun } from \\\"../../core/dry-run.js\\\";\\nimport type { ServerContext } from \\\"../index.js\\\";\\n\\n// ─── Request/Response Schemas ────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\ninterface SubmitBody {\\n  /** Natural language prompt — also accepted as \\\"input\\\" */\\n  prompt?: string;\\n  input?: string;\\n  /** Repo path to operate on */\\n  repoPath?: string;\\n  exclusions?: string[];\\n  quality_bar?: \\\"minimal\\\" | \\\"standard\\\" | \\\"hardened\\\";\\n}\\n\\ninterface TaskParams {\\n  id: string;\\n}\\n\\ninterface LoquiBody {\\n  question: string;\\n  repoPath: string;\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Unified Loqui route request body. A single freeform input stream\\n * that the classifier will route to build / answer / resume /\\n * clarify. The `context` field is optional — the UI passes it when\\n * it knows about a prior run so the classifier can emit resume_run\\n * or status intents safely (see core/loqui-intent.ts Rule B).\\n */\\ninterface LoquiUnifiedBody {\\n  input: string;\\n  repoPath: string;\\n  context?: LoquiIntentContext;\\n}\\n\\n// ─── In-memory run tracker (bridges POST → Coordinator → WS) ────────\\n\\ninterface TrackedRun {\\n  taskId: string;\\n  runId: string;\\n  status: \\\"queued\\\" | \\\"running\\\" | \\\"complete\\\" | \\\"failed\\\" | \\\"cancelled\\\";\\n  prompt: string;\\n  submittedAt: string;\\n  completedAt: string | null;\\n  receipt: unknown | null;\\n  error: string | null;\\n}\\n\\nconst trackedRuns = new Map<string, TrackedRun>();\\n\\nexport function getTrackedRun(taskId: string): TrackedRun | undefined {\\n  return trackedRuns.get(taskId);\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Snapshot of every tracked run, newest first. Read-only — returns\\n * a fresh array so callers can iterate / sort / slice without\\n * touching the internal Map. Used by the external API layer\\n * (Metrics + External API v1) so /metrics, /runs, and /runs/:id\\n * can aggregate across the same registry that /tasks writes to,\\n * without importing the private Map or creating a second source\\n * of truth.\\n */\\nexport function getAllTrackedRuns(): readonly TrackedRun[] {\\n  const out = [...trackedRuns.values()];\\n  out.sort((a, b) => (a.submittedAt < b.submittedAt ? 1 : -1));\\n  return out;\\n}\\n\\n/** Exported type for the external API routes. */\\nexport type { TrackedRun };\\n\\n/**\\n * Result from submitBuildTask — may be a running build, a clarification\\n * request, or a decomposition plan.\\n */\\ntype BuildSubmitResult =\\n  | { kind: \\\"running\\\"; taskId: string; runId: string; prompt: string; repoPath: string | null }\\n  | { kind: \\\"needs_clarification\\\"; question: string }\\n  | { kind: \\\"needs_decomposition\\\"; taskId: string; plan: unknown; message: string };\\n\\n/**\\n * Shared build-submit helper. Dispatches a build task through the\\n * Coordinator (via submitWithGates) and registers the tracked run state.\\n * Used by both the legacy POST /tasks handler and the new unified POST\\n * /tasks/loqui path so we get exactly one code path for \\\"start a build.\\\"\\n *\\n * Now returns a discriminated union so callers can handle clarification\\n * and decomposition gates without starting execution.\\n */\\nasync function submitBuildTask(\\n  ctx: ServerContext,\\n  prompt: string,\\n  repoPath: string | undefined,\\n  exclusions: string[] | undefined,\\n): Promise<BuildSubmitResult> {\\n  const taskId = `task_${randomUUID().slice(0, 8)}`;\\n  const runId = randomUUID();\\n  const submittedAt = new Date().toISOString();\\n\\n  // Run pre-execution gates (ambiguity + decomposition)\\n  const gateResult = await ctx.coordinator.submitWithGates({\\n    runId,\\n    input: prompt,\\n    exclusions,\\n    ...(repoPath ? { projectRoot: repoPath } : {}),\\n  });\\n\\n  if (gateResult.kind === \\\"needs_clarification\\\") {\\n    console.log(`[tasks] clarification needed for prompt: \\\"${prompt.slice(0, 80)}\\\"`);\\n    return { kind: \\\"needs_clarification\\\", question: gateResult.question };\\n  }\\n\\n  if (gateResult.kind === \\\"needs_decomposition\\\") {\\n    console.log(`[tasks] decomposition needed — ${(gateResult.plan as any).waves?.length ?? 0} wave(s)`);\\n    return {\\n      kind: \\\"needs_decomposition\\\",\\n      taskId: gateResult.taskId,\\n      plan: gateResult.plan,\\n      message: gateResult.message,\\n    };\\n  }\\n\\n  // Gate passed — execution started\\n  const tracked: TrackedRun = {\\n    taskId,\\n    runId,\\n    status: \\\"queued\\\",\\n    prompt,\\n    submittedAt,\\n    completedAt: null,\\n    receipt: null,\\n    error: null,\\n  };\\n  trackedRuns.set(taskId, tracked);\\n  await ctx.receiptStore.registerTask({\\n    taskId,\\n    runId,\\n    prompt: tracked.prompt,\\n    submittedAt,\\n  });\\n  tracked.status = \\\"running\\\";\\n  void ctx.receiptStore.updateTask(taskId, { status: \\\"running\\\" }).catch((err: unknown) =>\\n    console.error(\\\"[tasks] receiptStore update failed:\\\", err),\\n  );\\n\\n  ctx.eventBus.emit({\\n    type: \\\"run_started\\\",\\n    payload: { taskId, runId, prompt: tracked.prompt, status: \\\"running\\\", repoPath: repoPath ?? null },\\n  });\\n\\n  console.log(`[tasks] coordinator executing for taskId=${taskId} (projectRoot=${repoPath ?? \\\"(default)\\\"})...`);\\n\\n  // The receipt promise is already running from submitWithGates\\n  gateResult.receipt.then((receipt) => {\\n    console.log(`[tasks] coordinator.submit resolved: taskId=${taskId}, verdict=${receipt.verdict}, cost=$${receipt.totalCost.estimatedCostUsd}`);\\n    tracked.runId = receipt.runId;\\n    tracked.status = receipt.verdict === \\\"success\\\" ? \\\"complete\\\" : receipt.verdict === \\\"aborted\\\" ? \\\"cancelled\\\" : \\\"failed\\\";\\n    tracked.completedAt = new Date().toISOString();\\n    tracked.receipt = receipt;\\n    void ctx.receiptStore.updateTask(taskId, {\\n      runId: receipt.runId,\\n      status: tracked.status,\\n      completedAt: tracked.completedAt,\\n      error: null,\\n    }).catch((err: unknown) =>\\n      console.error(\\\"[tasks] receiptStore update failed:\\\", err),\\n    );\\n\\n    ctx.eventBus.emit({\\n      type: \\\"run_complete\\\",\\n      payload: {\\n        taskId,\\n        runId: receipt.runId,\\n        verdict: receipt.verdict,\\n        totalCostUsd: receipt.totalCost.estimatedCostUsd,\\n        durationMs: receipt.durationMs,\\n        executionVerified: receipt.executionVerified,\\n        executionReason: receipt.executionGateReason,\\n      },\\n    });\\n\\n    ctx.eventBus.emit({\\n      type: \\\"run_receipt\\\",\\n      payload: { taskId, runId: receipt.runId, receiptId: receipt.id, receipt },\\n    });\\n  }).catch((err) => {\\n    console.error(\\\"═══ COORDINATOR SUBMIT FAILED ═══\\\");\\n    console.error(\\\"taskId:\\\", taskId);\\n    console.error(\\\"prompt:\\\", tracked.prompt.slice(0, 200));\\n    console.error(\\\"repoPath:\\\", repoPath ?? \\\"(default)\\\");\\n    console.error(\\\"error:\\\", err instanceof Error ? err.message : err);\\n    console.error(\\\"stack:\\\", err instanceof Error ? err.stack : \\\"\\\");\\n    console.error(\\\"═════════════════════════════════\\\");\\n\\n    tracked.status = \\\"failed\\\";\\n    tracked.completedAt = new Date().toISOString();\\n    tracked.error = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);\\n    void ctx.receiptStore.updateTask(taskId, {\\n      status: \\\"failed\\\",\\n      completedAt: tracked.completedAt,\\n      error: tracked.error,\\n    }).catch((err: unknown) =>\\n      console.error(\\\"[tasks] receiptStore update failed:\\\", err),\\n    );\\n\\n    ctx.eventBus.emit({\\n      type: \\\"run_complete\\\",\\n      payload: {\\n        taskId,\\n        runId,\\n        verdict: \\\"failed\\\",\\n        error: tracked.error,\\n        executionVerified: false,\\n      },\\n    });\\n  });\\n\\n  return { kind: \\\"running\\\", taskId, runId, prompt, repoPath: repoPath ?? null };\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Find the most recent tracked run so the unified Loqui route can\\n * reconstruct the \\\"continuation\\\" prompt for a resume_run intent.\\n * Returns null when the session has no prior runs — the classifier\\n * already guards against this but the handler double-checks so a\\n * stale client context cannot spawn a resume against nothing.\\n */\\nfunction findLatestTrackedRun(): TrackedRun | null {\\n  let latest: TrackedRun | null = null;\\n  for (const run of trackedRuns.values()) {\\n    if (!latest) {\\n      latest = run;\\n      continue;\\n    }\\n    if (run.submittedAt > latest.submittedAt) {\\n      latest = run;\\n    }\\n  }\\n  return latest;\\n}\\n\\nasync function findLatestKnownRun(ctx: ServerContext): Promise<{\\n  taskId: string | null;\\n  runId: string;\\n  prompt: string;\\n  status: string;\\n} | null> {\\n  const
